governing_requirement: The producer receipt carries an exact named and hash-bound use_eligibility observation, Main preserves but discards it as decision authority, Main independently recomputes normalized intended-use eligibility, and any disagreement fails closed.

prerequisites.disposition: Hold use-eligibility implementation until the normalized actual-byte consumption decision is adopted.

existing_enforcement.evidence: mask_acquisition_receipt.schema.json requires policy_id, policy_sha256, required_authority_state, exact_use_scope, eligible, and non-empty unique reasons.
validate_mask_acquisition_receipt rejects eligible=true when the result did not succeed or observed authority is below the producer-declared minimum.
MF-P6-09.03 supplies a self-hashed deterministic authority policy and decision, separates access from authority and training truth, and prevents Main/LLM/provider authority grants.

trusted_resign_probes.probe: Replace policy_id and policy_sha256 with an arbitrary LLM-asserted identity and trusted re-sign the receipt.
trusted_resign_probes.interpretation: The receipt validator treats policy identity and hash as assertions and does not resolve or recompute a named policy.

trusted_resign_probes.probe: Replace exact_use_scope=production_conditioning with diagnostic and trusted re-sign the receipt.
trusted_resign_probes.interpretation: Eligibility is not bound to the exact requested/certificate use scope.

trusted_resign_probes.probe: Replace reasons with provider says globally certified and trusted re-sign the receipt.
trusted_resign_probes.interpretation: Reasons are free-form and no closed deterministic decision proves their source.

trusted_resign_probes.probe: Set eligible=false with an arbitrary disagreement reason on an otherwise eligible certified receipt and trusted re-sign it.
trusted_resign_probes.interpretation: No producer recomputation exists, so producer-policy disagreement cannot be detected or journaled.

missing_proof: No producer-owned evaluator loads a named consumer-use policy, verifies its canonical self-hash, normalizes factual receipt/request/certificate/consumption evidence, and deterministically derives the wire observation.
The declared required_authority_state is itself trusted; it is not recomputed from exact_use_scope and the pinned policy.
Eligibility is not proven against certificate permitted uses, exact certificate scope, partial output/label/owner coverage, current revocation, QA, transform, or actual-byte consumption result.
The receipt's eligible boolean and reasons are not checked against a recomputed producer decision.
There is no closed reason-code ordering or prohibition on LLM/provider-authored eligibility assertions beyond the separate authority lattice.
No producer golden vectors expose a hash-bound normalized input and expected observation for independent Main recomputation.
No cross-project mismatch fixture proves producer/Main disagreement fails closed or that Main's compatibility boolean is derived only from its complete independent decision.

implementation_packet.objective: Implement the producer-owned deterministic use-eligibility observation and golden vectors over the adopted actual-byte consumption decision without modifying frozen v1 wire contracts.
implementation_packet.path_relationship: Allowed paths are absent, clean, and disjoint from frozen 08.05/08.06/08.07/09.01 scopes plus prepared 09.02 and 09.04 allowed paths. Candidate paths are read-only; validation.py is already dirty in the frozen 08.05 scope and must not be touched.
acceptance_criteria: Load one closed versioned use policy and verify policy_id, canonical self-hash, exact allowed scopes, required authority floors, and closed ordered reason codes before evaluation.

Normalize only factual request, receipt, certificate, authority-lattice, actual-byte consumption, QA, transform, lineage, and current revocation evidence; producer eligible/reasons fields are never decision inputs.
Recompute required authority from the exact use scope and policy rather than trusting the receipt declaration.

Require exact source, owner, intent, label/region, ontology, transform, artifact, certificate, permitted-use, validity, revocation, QA, and consumption scope for every required output; a global certified shortcut or partial scope cannot pass.
Only the MaskFactory producer evaluator may emit the observation; LLM, VLM, provider, Main, and free-form caller roles fail closed and cannot grant authority.

Produce the exact frozen-v1 use_eligibility wire object plus an internal decision record binding policy identity/hash, normalized facts, eligibility, deterministic reasons, and canonical decision hash.
Validate a supplied receipt observation by recomputation; any policy, minimum, scope, boolean, reason, fact, or decision-hash mismatch fails closed and is journal-ready.

Publish canonical producer golden vectors covering eligible, insufficient authority, exact-scope mismatch, partial certificate scope, revoked/stale certificate, QA veto, transform mismatch, consumption rejection, global-shortcut attempt, and LLM/provider assertion.
Frozen v1 contracts, bridge catalog, semantic profile, fixtures, validation.py, and all held worker paths remain byte-unchanged.
implementation_packet.consumer_acceptance_dependency: ComfyUI Main must independently implement the same normalized decision from the pinned policy/vectors, preserve but discard the producer observation as authority, derive any compatibility alias solely from its decision, and prove producer/Main disagreement fails closed. MaskFactory cannot claim MF-P6-09.05 complete until that exact cross-project evidence is returned.

claim_boundary: This audit proves the producer-policy enforcement gap and prepares a disjoint implementation unit. It does not implement producer or Main recomputation, satisfy cross-project disagreement tests, or claim MF-P6-09.05 completion.
